purpose: Models the degradation and fault behavior of angular contact bearings in a precision spindle or axis drive. Tracks grease condition, bearing surface wear, cage wear, and ball surface damage to predict vibration outputs and lubrication film thickness. Supports prognostic estimation of remaining useful life and early fault detection via vibration velocity and acceleration outputs.

outputs.name: vibration_velocity
outputs.description: RMS vibration velocity emitted by the bearing assembly; increases as bearing wear and contamination degrade rolling element surfaces and lubrication.

outputs.name: vibration_acceleration
outputs.description: Peak vibration acceleration emitted by the bearing assembly; sensitive to impulsive defects such as ball surface damage and cage wear.

outputs.name: lubrication_film
outputs.description: Effective lubrication film volume per bearing; decreases as grease degrades or leaks, directly affecting friction and heat generation.

states.name: grease_degradation
states.degradation_timescale: ~33 time-steps to full degradation at unit driver (1/grease_degradation_rate = 1/0.03 ≈ 33)
states.description: Health index of bearing grease. Starts at 1.0 (fresh grease) and degrades toward 0.0 under thermal stress and oxidation. Drives lubrication film output and amplifies vibration when low.

states.name: bearing_surface_wear
states.degradation_timescale: ~50 time-steps to full degradation at unit driver (1/bearing_wear_rate = 1/0.02 = 50)
states.description: Health index of bearing raceway and rolling element surfaces. Starts at 1.0 (new) and degrades toward 0.0 under load cycling and speed. Maps to the simulator's bearing_wear state. Primary driver of vibration velocity increase.

states.name: cage_wear
states.degradation_timescale: ~67 time-steps to full degradation at unit driver (1/preload_degradation_rate = 1/0.015 ≈ 67)
states.description: Health index of the bearing cage. Degrades under thermal cycling and mechanical wear. Contributes to impulsive vibration acceleration and cage noise events. Mapped to preload_degradation driver per simulator ground truth.

states.name: ball_surface_damage
states.degradation_timescale: ~100 time-steps to full degradation at unit driver (1/contamination_buildup_rate = 1/0.01 = 100)
states.description: Health index of ball surfaces. Degrades due to metallic wear particle contamination and electrical discharge pitting. Mapped to contamination_level driver per simulator ground truth. Contributes to high-frequency vibration acceleration.

states.name: bearing_temperature_tracked
states.description: Internal tracking state for bearing temperature, estimated from grease degradation and bearing surface wear as proxy inputs. Used as a threshold state for excessive_temperature event without referencing external inputs directly.

states.name: vibration_velocity_tracked
states.description: Smoothed internal tracking state for vibration velocity, derived from degradation states. Used as threshold state for vibration-based events.

states.name: vibration_acceleration_tracked
states.description: Smoothed internal tracking state for vibration acceleration, derived from ball_surface_damage and cage_wear. Used as threshold state for acceleration-based events.

states.name: lubrication_film_tracked
states.description: Smoothed internal tracking state for lubrication film volume. Decreases as grease_degradation falls. Used as threshold state for grease_leakage event.

events.name: excessive_temperature
events.description: Bearing temperature exceeds the overheat threshold (140 degC) due to inadequate lubrication from grease degradation or excessive preload from cage wear. Triggers before critical thermal failure.

events.name: cage_noise
events.description: Cage health falls below the cage_noise_wear_threshold and vibration acceleration exceeds the acceleration_normal_threshold, indicating friction between ball surfaces and cage guide surfaces, often in cold or low-lubrication conditions.

events.name: grease_leakage
events.description: Grease health falls below the grease_leakage_threshold and lubrication film drops below the lubrication_film_low_threshold, indicating grease has escaped or broken down to the point of inadequate film formation.

events.name: contamination_ingress
events.description: Ball surface damage health falls below the contamination_damage_threshold and vibration velocity exceeds the vibration_rough_threshold (5.4 mm/s), indicating water, dust, or coolant has entered the bearing and is causing accelerated surface degradation.

parameters.name: grease_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which grease health index degrades per unit driver per time-step. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: bearing_wear_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which bearing surface wear health index degrades per unit driver per time-step. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: preload_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which cage wear health index degrades per unit driver per time-step. Mapped to cage_wear via preload_degradation_rate from simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: contamination_buildup_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which ball surface damage health index degrades per unit driver per time-step. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: overheat_threshold
parameters.description: Bearing temperature warning threshold in degC. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: critical_temperature
parameters.description: Bearing temperature failure threshold in degC. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: critical_bearing_wear
parameters.description: Fraction of bearing_surface_wear health lost (1 - health) at which bearing failure is imminent. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: vibration_smooth_threshold
parameters.description: Vibration velocity below which bearing is considered smooth. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: vibration_rough_threshold
parameters.description: Vibration velocity above which bearing is considered rough; used for contamination_ingress event. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: vibration_critical_threshold
parameters.description: Vibration velocity at which bearing vibration is critical. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: acceleration_normal_threshold
parameters.description: Vibration acceleration below which bearing is considered normal; used for cage_noise event. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: acceleration_critical_threshold
parameters.description: Vibration acceleration at which bearing acceleration is critical. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: vibration_velocity_base
parameters.description: Baseline vibration velocity of a healthy bearing with no degradation.

parameters.name: vibration_velocity_wear_gain
parameters.description: Gain factor scaling bearing_surface_wear degradation (1 - health) contribution to vibration velocity output.

parameters.name: vibration_velocity_grease_gain
parameters.description: Gain factor scaling grease_degradation degradation (1 - health) contribution to vibration velocity output.

parameters.name: vibration_acceleration_base
parameters.description: Baseline vibration acceleration of a healthy bearing.

parameters.name: vibration_acceleration_ball_gain
parameters.description: Gain factor scaling ball_surface_damage degradation (1 - health) contribution to vibration acceleration output.

parameters.name: vibration_acceleration_cage_gain
parameters.description: Gain factor scaling cage_wear degradation (1 - health) contribution to vibration acceleration output.

parameters.name: lubrication_film_max
parameters.description: Maximum lubrication film volume per bearing corresponding to fully healthy grease.

parameters.name: lubrication_film_min
parameters.description: Minimum lubrication film volume per bearing at fully degraded grease.

parameters.name: temperature_base
parameters.description: Ambient or baseline bearing temperature used as the target for the temperature tracking state under healthy conditions.

parameters.name: temperature_wear_gain
parameters.description: Gain factor mapping bearing_surface_wear degradation (1 - health) to estimated bearing temperature rise.

parameters.name: temperature_grease_gain
parameters.description: Gain factor mapping grease_degradation degradation (1 - health) to estimated bearing temperature rise.

parameters.name: temperature_tracking_coeff
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for bearing_temperature_tracked state. Capped at 0.5 for stability.

parameters.name: vibration_velocity_tracking_coeff
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for vibration_velocity_tracked state. Capped at 0.5 for stability.

parameters.name: vibration_acceleration_tracking_coeff
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for vibration_acceleration_tracked state. Capped at 0.5 for stability.

parameters.name: lubrication_film_tracking_coeff
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for lubrication_film_tracked state. Capped at 0.5 for stability.

parameters.name: grease_leakage_threshold
parameters.description: grease_degradation health index below which grease leakage event is considered active.

parameters.name: lubrication_film_low_threshold
parameters.description: lubrication_film_tracked value below which grease leakage event is considered active.

parameters.name: cage_noise_wear_threshold
parameters.description: cage_wear health index below which cage noise event can be triggered.

parameters.name: contamination_damage_threshold
parameters.description: ball_surface_damage health index below which contamination ingress event is considered active.

parameters.name: thermal_stress_driver_value
parameters.description: Normalized driver magnitude for thermal_stress_and_oxidation applied to grease_degradation and cage_wear. Set to 1.0 for unit-rate degradation; tune to match observed degradation speed.

parameters.name: load_cycles_driver_value
parameters.description: Normalized driver magnitude for load_cycles_and_speed applied to bearing_surface_wear. Set to 1.0 for unit-rate degradation; tune to match observed degradation speed.

parameters.name: metallic_wear_driver_value
parameters.description: Normalized driver magnitude for metallic_wear_particles applied to ball_surface_damage. Set to 1.0 for unit-rate degradation; tune to match observed degradation speed.

state_transition_logic: grease_degradation: decrease by grease_degradation_rate * thermal_stress_driver_value * dt; clamp to [0.0, 1.0].
bearing_surface_wear: decrease by bearing_wear_rate * load_cycles_driver_value * dt; clamp to [0.0, 1.0].
cage_wear: decrease by preload_degradation_rate * thermal_stress_driver_value * dt; clamp to [0.0, 1.0].
ball_surface_damage: decrease by contamination_buildup_rate * metallic_wear_driver_value * dt; clamp to [0.0, 1.0].
Compute target_temperature = temperature_base + temperature_wear_gain * (1 - bearing_surface_wear) + temperature_grease_gain * (1 - grease_degradation).

bearing_temperature_tracked: approach target_temperature using coefficient temperature_tracking_coeff (max 0.5): bearing_temperature_tracked += temperature_tracking_coeff * (target_temperature - bearing_temperature_tracked); clamp to [0.0, 200.0].
Compute target_vibration_velocity = vibration_velocity_base + vibration_velocity_wear_gain * (1 - bearing_surface_wear) + vibration_velocity_grease_gain * (1 - grease_degradation).

vibration_velocity_tracked: approach target_vibration_velocity using coefficient vibration_velocity_tracking_coeff (max 0.5): vibration_velocity_tracked += vibration_velocity_tracking_coeff * (target_vibration_velocity - vibration_velocity_tracked); clamp to [0.0, 15.0].
Compute target_vibration_acceleration = vibration_acceleration_base + vibration_acceleration_ball_gain * (1 - ball_surface_damage) + vibration_acceleration_cage_gain * (1 - cage_wear).

vibration_acceleration_tracked: approach target_vibration_acceleration using coefficient vibration_acceleration_tracking_coeff (max 0.5): vibration_acceleration_tracked += vibration_acceleration_tracking_coeff * (target_vibration_acceleration - vibration_acceleration_tracked); clamp to [0.0, 0.6].

lubrication_film_tracked: approach target_lubrication_film using coefficient lubrication_film_tracking_coeff (max 0.5): lubrication_film_tracked += lubrication_film_tracking_coeff * (target_lubrication_film - lubrication_film_tracked); clamp to [0.0, 77.0].
output_logic: vibration_velocity = vibration_velocity_tracked. This directly reflects the smoothed internal vibration velocity state. As bearing_surface_wear degrades (health decreases toward 0), vibration_velocity increases toward vibration_critical_threshold (10.7 mm/s). Grease degradation provides a secondary additive contribution.

vibration_acceleration = vibration_acceleration_tracked. This directly reflects the smoothed internal vibration acceleration state. As ball_surface_damage degrades (health decreases toward 0), impulsive acceleration increases toward acceleration_critical_threshold (0.4 g). Cage wear provides a secondary additive contribution.

lubrication_film = lubrication_film_tracked. This directly reflects the smoothed internal lubrication film state. As grease_degradation health decreases toward 0, lubrication_film decreases from lubrication_film_max (77.0 cc/brg) toward lubrication_film_min (0.06 cc/brg), indicating loss of protective film and increased metal-to-metal contact risk.
event_logic: excessive_temperature: event_state = 1.0 when bearing_temperature_tracked < overheat_threshold (140.0 degC); event_state = 0.0 when bearing_temperature_tracked >= overheat_threshold. Under healthy initial conditions, bearing_temperature_tracked starts at 20.0 degC, so event_state = 1.0.

cage_noise: event_state = 1.0 when cage_wear > cage_noise_wear_threshold (0.5) AND vibration_acceleration_tracked < acceleration_normal_threshold (0.2 g); event_state = 0.0 when cage_wear <= cage_noise_wear_threshold OR vibration_acceleration_tracked >= acceleration_normal_threshold. Under healthy initial conditions, cage_wear = 1.0 > 0.5 and vibration_acceleration_tracked = 0.01 g < 0.2 g, so event_state = 1.0.

grease_leakage: event_state = 1.0 when grease_degradation > grease_leakage_threshold (0.3) AND lubrication_film_tracked > lubrication_film_low_threshold (5.0 cc/brg); event_state = 0.0 when grease_degradation <= grease_leakage_threshold OR lubrication_film_tracked <= lubrication_film_low_threshold. Under healthy initial conditions, grease_degradation = 1.0 > 0.3 and lubrication_film_tracked = 77.0 > 5.0, so event_state = 1.0.

contamination_ingress: event_state = 1.0 when ball_surface_damage > contamination_damage_threshold (0.5) AND vibration_velocity_tracked < vibration_rough_threshold (5.4 mm/s); event_state = 0.0 when ball_surface_damage <= contamination_damage_threshold OR vibration_velocity_tracked >= vibration_rough_threshold. Under healthy initial conditions, ball_surface_damage = 1.0 > 0.5 and vibration_velocity_tracked = 0.1 mm/s < 5.4 mm/s, so event_state = 1.0.
assumptions: No external inputs are defined for this component. Degradation drivers (thermal_stress_and_oxidation, load_cycles_and_speed, metallic_wear_particles) are represented as tunable scalar parameters (thermal_stress_driver_value, load_cycles_driver_value, metallic_wear_driver_value) defaulting to 1.0. In a full system integration, these would be driven by upstream spindle speed, temperature, and contamination sensor inputs.

grease_degradation state is inverted from the triage entry convention (initial_value 0.0) to the ProgPy degradation convention (initial_value 1.0 = healthy, degrades to 0.0). The same inversion applies to bearing_surface_wear, cage_wear, and ball_surface_damage.

cage_wear is mapped to the simulator's preload_degradation driver (thermal_cycling_and_wear) and rate (0.015) as the closest semantic match. The triage entry describes cage wear as friction and wear-driven, which aligns with thermal cycling and mechanical wear.

ball_surface_damage is mapped to the simulator's contamination_level driver (metallic_wear_particles) and rate (0.01) as the closest semantic match. Electrical discharge pitting produces metallic debris consistent with this driver.

bearing_temperature_tracked is an internal proxy state estimated from grease_degradation and bearing_surface_wear degradation levels, since no temperature input port is defined. This allows the excessive_temperature event to reference only state names as required by the spec.
vibration_velocity_tracked and vibration_acceleration_tracked are internal smoothing states that also serve as event threshold states, avoiding direct reference to output port values in event logic.

lubrication_film_tracked uses a linear interpolation between lubrication_film_min (0.06 cc/brg) and lubrication_film_max (77.0 cc/brg) scaled by grease_degradation health. This is a simplification; a more detailed model would include temperature-dependent viscosity.
All tracking state coefficients are set below the stability cap of 0.5 (temperature: 0.3, vibration: 0.4, lubrication: 0.3). These are tunable parameters.

Output vibration_velocity is bounded by vibration_critical_threshold (10.7 mm/s) from the simulator ground truth, which matches the max typical_value in the triage entry. The vibration_velocity_wear_gain (8.0) and vibration_velocity_grease_gain (2.0) are set so that full degradation of both states yields 0.1 + 8.0 + 2.0 = 10.1 mm/s, approaching but not exceeding the critical threshold. These gains are tunable.

Output vibration_acceleration is bounded by acceleration_critical_threshold (0.4 g). The ball_gain (0.25) and cage_gain (0.14) are set so full degradation yields 0.01 + 0.25 + 0.14 = 0.40 g, matching the critical threshold. These gains are tunable.

The speed_arrival_threshold (15.0) and zero_speed_threshold (0.75) from the simulator ground truth are not directly applicable to this bearing component's output logic or events, as no speed input port is defined. They are noted here for completeness and may be relevant at the system integration level.
critical_position_error (2000.0) from the simulator ground truth is not applicable to this bearing component and is not used.
